preview_text:
The model favors Iran at 49%, while Egypt's win probability stands at 22%. Iran enters as clear statistical favorite on the neutral ground, leaving Egypt with a modest chance of victory and substantial ground to make up.

The draw emerges as a realistic outcome at 30% under the model, sitting between the two outright win probabilities. This three-way split suggests a competitive match without an overwhelming dominance scenario; the gap between Iran's lead and Egypt's floor is meaningful but not decisive, and neither side faces prohibitive odds.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge sharply. Books price Egypt's win at 42%, a 20-point gap above the model, while pricing Iran's win at 27%—a 21-point gap below. The model sees this as Iran-leaning; the market treats it as nearly a toss-up. Draw probabilities align closely at 30–31%. Polymarket shows equal thirds across all outcomes. The dashboard makes no claim about which reading is correct, only that material disagreement exists.
